title: picrasma quassioides var. dasycarpa
intro: Picrasma quassioides var. dasycarpa, also known as dasycarp, is a plant species that belongs to the Phyllanthaceae family. The medicinal properties of dasycarp are attributed to its rich content of alkaloids, flavonoids, and tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is known for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. Dasycarp is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in traditional medicine, although its modern applications and dosages should be researched and confirmed by qualified healthcare professionals.
